comments.body: You have reached your Codex usage limits for code reviews. You can see your limits in the [Codex usage dashboard](https://chatgpt.com/codex/settings/usage).

## Code Review

This pull request implements Phase 1 and Phase 2 of the live logs plan, introducing new observability API endpoints for task run summaries, logs, and diagnostics. The ManagedRunRecord schema was updated with specific artifact references and log offsets, and the supervisor now populates these fields. Review feedback identifies critical path traversal vulnerabilities in the file-serving endpoints that require path validation. Additionally, the logic for retrieving merged logs needs to be corrected to use the dedicated artifact reference, and inline imports should be moved to the top of the file to adhere to PEP 8 standards.

![security-critical](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/security-critical.svg) ![critical](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/critical.svg)

There is a path traversal vulnerability here. The `target_ref` is read from a file store and used to construct a file path without proper validation. An attacker who can control the content of the managed run record could set `target_ref` to a value like `../../../../etc/passwd` to read arbitrary files from the server.

You should validate that the resolved path of the artifact is within the expected artifacts root directory before serving the file. A similar check is performed in `moonmind/workflows/temporal/runtime/store.py` using `is_relative_to`, which you could reuse here.

```suggestion
    artifacts_root = Path(_get_agent_runtime_artifacts_root()).resolve()
    artifact_path = (artifacts_root / target_ref).resolve()
    
    if not artifact_path.is_relative_to(artifacts_root) or not artifact_path.is_file():
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND,
            detail=f"Log file {target_ref} does not exist",
        )
```

![security-critical](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/security-critical.svg) ![critical](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/critical.svg)

Similar to the `stream_task_run_log` endpoint, this endpoint has a path traversal vulnerability. The `record.diagnostics_ref` is used to construct a file path without validating that it is within the expected artifacts directory. This could allow an attacker to read arbitrary files from the system.

Please add validation to ensure the resolved path is within the artifacts root directory.

```suggestion
    artifacts_root = Path(_get_agent_runtime_artifacts_root()).resolve()
    artifact_path = (artifacts_root / record.diagnostics_ref).resolve()
    
    if not artifact_path.is_relative_to(artifacts_root) or not artifact_path.is_file():
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND,
            detail=f"Diagnostics file {record.diagnostics_ref} does not exist",
        )
```

![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

The logic for handling a `merged` log stream appears to be incorrect. When `stream_name` is `"merged"`, `target_stream` is set to `"stdout"`, causing this endpoint to return the `stdout` log instead of a merged log.
The `ManagedRunRecord` schema includes a `merged_log_artifact_ref` field, which should be used when `stream_name` is `"merged"`. If a merged log is not available, it would be better to return a 404 error rather than silently serving the `stdout` log.

```suggestion
    store = ManagedRunStore(_get_agent_runtime_store_root())
    record = store.load(str(id))
    
    if not record:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND,
            detail="Observability record not found for this task run",
        )

    if stream_name == "merged":
        target_ref = record.merged_log_artifact_ref
    else:
        target_ref = getattr(record, f"{stream_name}_artifact_ref", None)
    
    if not target_ref:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND,
            detail=f"{stream_name} log artifact not found",
        )
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

According to PEP 8, imports should be at the top of the file. Moving these imports (`os`, `pathlib`, `FileResponse`, `ManagedRunStore`) to the top of `api_service/api/routers/task_runs.py` will improve code readability and maintainability.

## Unused local variable Variable response is not used. --- In general, to fix an "unused local variable" you either remove the variable (and, if appropriate, any associated expression) or start using it meaningfully. Here, the GET request must still be performed to exercise the endpoint, but the result is not inspected; we only care that FileResponse is called with certain arguments. The best minimal fix is to keep the test_client.get(...) call for its side effects while dropping the unused variable binding.

Concretely, in tests/unit/api/routers/test_task_runs.py, within test_stream_task_run_log_returns_file_response, change the line:

response = test_client.get(f"/api/task-runs/{uuid4()}/logs/stdout")

to:

test_client.get(f"/api/task-runs/{uuid4()}/logs/stdout")

No imports or additional definitions are required.

These observability endpoints allow any authenticated user to fetch any run’s record/logs as long as they know the UUID. Other routers enforce repository-scoped access (e.g. `api_service/api/routers/automation.py:_ensure_run_access` around lines 176-191). Please add an equivalent authorization check for task-run observability (or explicitly restrict these routes to admin/superuser) before serving file-backed artifacts.

`ManagedRunStore.load()` and `Path.exists()/is_file()` are synchronous filesystem operations executed inside `async def` endpoints. Under load this can block the event loop. Consider either (1) making these routes sync (`def`) so FastAPI runs them in a threadpool, or (2) wrapping the file/store calls in `asyncio.to_thread(...)`.

The new observability tests patch `FileResponse` and only assert that it was called, without asserting the endpoint returns a real `Response` (status code, headers like `Cache-Control`, etc.). Consider returning an actual `Response` instance from the `FileResponse` patch so the test can assert the HTTP behavior, or alternatively assert on headers/content-type from the `client.get(...)` response.

`get_task_run_diagnostics` has branches for (a) diagnostics ref present but file missing and (b) successful file response, but the added unit tests only cover the missing-ref case. Please add tests that validate the 404 when the diagnostics file path does not exist, and the happy-path response when it does (including content-type).

The Phase 2 checklist marks tail/pagination/range behavior and “large artifacts” testing as complete, but the implemented `/logs/{stream_name}` route currently returns the full file and the added unit tests don’t cover range/tail semantics. Please either update the checklist to reflect what’s actually implemented, or implement the remaining behaviors before checking these off.

Typo in comment: `# Capabilty metadata ...` should be `# Capability metadata ...`.
```suggestion
    # Capability metadata indicating if live streaming is supported for this run
```

`stream_name == "merged"` currently maps to `stdout_artifact_ref` (`target_stream = "stdout" if stream_name == "merged" ...`). This makes `/logs/merged` return stdout instead of a merged log. Use `record.merged_log_artifact_ref` when `stream_name == "merged"` (and return 404 if it's missing), or drop "merged" from the allowed list until a merged artifact is actually produced.
```suggestion

    store = ManagedRunStore(_get_agent_runtime_store_root())
    record = store.load(str(id))

    if not record:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND,
            detail="Observability record not found for this task run",
        )

    if stream_name == "merged":
        target_ref = getattr(record, "merged_log_artifact_ref", None)
        missing_detail = "merged log artifact not found"
    else:
        target_ref = getattr(record, f"{stream_name}_artifact_ref", None)
        missing_detail = f"{stream_name} log artifact not found"

    if not target_ref:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND,
            detail=missing_detail,
        )

    # The LogStreamer returns references like '<run_id>/stdout.log'
    artifact_path = Path(_get_agent_runtime_artifacts_root()) / target_ref

    if not artifact_path.exists() or not artifact_path.is_file():
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND,
            detail=f"Log file {target_ref} does not exist",
        )
```

Phase 2 requirements/docs reference tail/range semantics, but `/logs/{stream_name}` currently serves the entire artifact via `FileResponse` with no query params or Range support. Either implement byte-range/tail behavior here (and document/validate the params) or rename this to a full-download endpoint and add separate tail endpoints so the API matches the stated behavior.

The checklist items are written as ``- `[ ]` ...`` which renders as inline code, not an interactive Markdown task list. Use `- [ ]` / `- [x]` without backticks so the tasks render as checkboxes.
```suggestion
- [ ] Update `moonmind/schemas/agent_runtime_models.py` to add stdout/stderr artifact refs and last_log offsets.
- [ ] Update `moonmind/workflows/temporal/runtime/supervisor.py` and `store.py` to consume the new schema refs.
- [ ] Update `moonmind/proxy/routers/...` to expose endpoints `GET /api/task-runs/{id}/observability-summary`, `logs/stdout`, `logs/stderr`, etc.
- [ ] Write API unit tests.
- [ ] Mark completion in `docs/tmp/009-LiveLogsPlan.md`.
```

`artifact_path = Path(root) / target_ref` uses a storage ref from the run record without validating it stays within the artifacts root. If `target_ref` contains traversal (e.g. `../...`) this can read arbitrary files from the API host. Please resolve the candidate path and verify it is under the configured artifacts root (similar to `api_service/api/routers/automation.py:_resolve_artifact_file`), returning 400 on invalid paths.

## Pull request overview Implements Phase 2 “Live Logs” observability by extending the managed-run persistence model and exposing new task-run endpoints to fetch an observability summary and download log/diagnostics artifacts from the shared runtime volume. **Changes:** - Add `stdout_artifact_ref` / `stderr_artifact_ref` (and related metadata fields) to `ManagedRunRecord`, and update the supervisor to persist these refs on completion. - Introduce new API endpoints under `/api/task-runs/{id}/...` for `observability-summary`, `logs/{stream}`, and `diagnostics`. - Add unit tests for the new router endpoints and update Live Logs plan/spec artifacts and checklists. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 11 out of 11 changed files in this pull request and generated 10 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `api_service/api/routers/task_runs.py` | Adds new Observability API endpoints backed by `ManagedRunStore` and artifact files. | | `tests/unit/api/routers/test_task_runs.py` | Adds unit tests for the new observability endpoints and updates worker auth fixture fields. | | `moonmind/schemas/agent_runtime_models.py` | Extends `ManagedRunRecord` with stdout/stderr/merged refs and log offset/timestamp metadata. | | `moonmind/workflows/temporal/runtime/supervisor.py` | Writes stdout/stderr refs (and `last_log_at`) to managed run records and includes them in completion payload output refs. | | `docs/tmp/009-LiveLogsPlan.md` | Marks Phase 1/2 checklist items as completed. | | `specs/111-live-logs-phase-2/spec.md` | Adds Phase 2 specification (requirements + acceptance scenarios). | | `specs/111-live-logs-phase-2/plan.md` | Adds Phase 2 implementation plan describing model + API changes. | | `specs/111-live-logs-phase-2/tasks.md` | Adds Phase 2 task checklist. | | `specs/110-live-logs-phase-1/spec.md` | Adds Phase 1 specification (requirements + acceptance scenarios). | | `specs/110-live-logs-phase-1/plan.md` | Adds Phase 1 implementation plan/traceability notes. | | `specs/110-live-logs-phase-1/tasks.md` | Adds Phase 1 task checklist. |
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

## Unused import Import of 'AgentJobLiveSessionProvider' is not used. --- In general, an unused import should be removed from the file to avoid clutter and potential confusion. This keeps the codebase clean and can slightly improve import time and readability.

The single best fix here is to adjust the import statement on line 21 so that it only imports the models that are actually used in this file: AgentJobLiveSessionStatus, TaskRunLiveSession, and User. We will remove AgentJobLiveSessionProvider from the comma-separated list. No other code changes are required, and existing functionality will remain unchanged.

Concretely, in api_service/api/routers/task_runs.py, locate the from api_service.db.models import ... line that currently imports four names and modify it to import only the three used names. No new methods, imports, or definitions are needed.
